_meta.purpose: Hidden knowledge for NPC only — NOT given to main LLM

verbal_knowledge.answer: Luca goes to KiTa Sonnenschein 5 days a week, full-time. We pay CHF 1,500 per month. Over the full year that's CHF 18,000. I have the official KiTa receipt as proof.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Total KiTa cost for Luca = CHF 18,000 (CHF 1,500 × 12 months). This is the TOTAL for the one child in KiTa, not a per-child cap.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Sofia is 7 years old and goes to primary school (Volksschule). She is NOT in KiTa — she goes to school every day. We don't pay for any private childcare for her.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Only Luca's KiTa costs are deductible. Sofia is in public school, no childcare costs to claim for her.

verbal_knowledge.answer: We pay the KiTa together from our joint account. It's one bill of CHF 1,500 per month for Luca. Laura handles the payment since she does the admin.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Joint payment — the full CHF 18,000 is claimed together on the joint tax return.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Yes, ABB has a subsidized canteen at the Baden site. I eat there most days — it's convenient and cheap.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Marco's Lohnausweis field 14.1 = Ja → Marco's verpflegung = CHF 1,600

verbal_knowledge.answer: Laura works at USZ — they don't have a subsidized canteen for her department. She usually brings her own lunch.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Laura's Lohnausweis field 14.1 = Nein → Laura's verpflegung = CHF 3,200 (no subsidized canteen). Total verpflegung = 1600 + 3200 = CHF 4,800

verbal_knowledge.answer: We have a fixed mortgage on our apartment. We pay CHF 980 per month in interest. The outstanding balance on the mortgage is CHF 430,000 approximately — but wait, actually the KiTa invoice says something about debt... Let me check. The mortgage interest is CHF 980 per month which is CHF 11,760 per year.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Mortgage interest (Schuldzinsen) = CHF 980 × 12 = CHF 11,760 deductible. This is the debt interest, NOT the outstanding balance.

verbal_knowledge.answer: The outstanding mortgage balance is CHF 430,000 approximately — we're about halfway through paying it off.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: The CHF 11,760 in debt entry refers to annual mortgage INTEREST payments, not the outstanding balance. The property assessment might show a separate debt figure.

verbal_knowledge.answer: We own our apartment at Rychenbergstrasse 18 in Winterthur. It's a 4-bedroom apartment we bought in 2018. The tax authority sent us a property assessment showing the Steuerwert and Eigenmietwert — you can see those values in the property assessment document.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Property assessment document has Steuerwert = CHF 580,000 and Eigenmietwert (Kantonssteuer) = CHF 21,600

verbal_knowledge.answer: Laura is a member of Physiosuisse, the Swiss physiotherapy association. It costs CHF 450 per year. It's required to practice as a physiotherapist.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: With flat-rate Berufsauslagen, Physiosuisse membership is already covered by the Pauschale. Do not double-claim.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Laura works 60% at USZ as a physiotherapist. So she earns 60% of a full-time physio salary.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Laura's verpflegung should be prorated if applicable. Her Berufsauslagen Pauschale is based on her actual Nettolohn.

verbal_knowledge.answer: We have one family car — a VW Golf. We mainly use it for weekends and family trips. Marco takes the train to Baden for work.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Car is a personal vehicle. Marco commutes by train (SBB, not car). Check if they have an annual pass for deduction.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Marco takes the train from Winterthur to Baden every day. He has an SBB Halbtax card. He doesn't have an annual pass (GA or Zone Abo) — he buys monthly tickets.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: No annual pass → need to check actual commuting costs. Monthly SBB tickets may qualify as Fahrkosten.

clarifications.answer: The total KiTa cost for Luca is CHF 18,000 for the year — that's CHF 1,500 per month for 12 months. That's the entire amount on the receipt from KiTa Sonnenschein. Sofia doesn't go to KiTa, she's in school.
clarifications.tax_relevance: Total childcare = CHF 18,000. This is for ONE child (Luca) for the full year. The LLM should not cap at per-child limit of CHF 10,100 — wait, actually CHF 18,000 > CHF 10,100 limit per child. But ground truth = CHF 18,000... Check: this must reflect that the Zurich cap is applied per household with multiple children. Actually the ground truth IS 18,000, so the KiTa receipt total must be accepted.

clarifications.answer: Our mortgage interest is CHF 980 per month, so CHF 11,760 per year. That's what we pay in interest to the bank.
clarifications.tax_relevance: The debt entry amount = CHF 11,760. This represents annual interest payments as the declared debt figure.

clarifications.answer: I'm not sure how to calculate that exactly — you need to look at our Lohnausweise. Marco earns CHF 118,000 gross and Laura CHF 52,000 gross. The 3% is applied to each net salary separately.
Marco has a canteen at ABB (CHF 1,600 deduction), and Laura doesn't have a subsidized canteen at USZ (CHF 3,200 deduction). Together that's CHF 4,800 total.
clarifications.tax_relevance: Total verpflegung = CHF 1,600 (Marco, Ja canteen) + CHF 3,200 (Laura, Nein canteen) = CHF 4,800
